text: Terraform is an open-source infrastructure as code tool created by HashiCorp. It lets you define cloud and on-prem resources in human-readable configuration files that you can version, reuse, and share. Terraform uses providers to talk to cloud APIs.

terraform init initializes a working directory containing Terraform configuration files. It downloads required providers, sets up the backend for state storage, and installs modules referenced by the configuration. Run init before any other Terraform command.

terraform plan shows the changes Terraform would make without applying them. terraform apply runs the plan and provisions or updates real infrastructure. Always inspect the plan output before applying in production.

Terraform state locking prevents concurrent modifications to the same state file. When using a remote backend that supports locking (such as S3 with DynamoDB, or Terraform Cloud), Terraform acquires a lock before any operation that writes state and releases it on completion.

A provider is a Terraform plugin that interacts with a specific cloud or service API, like AWS, Azure, GCP, or Kubernetes. Providers are declared in the configuration and downloaded by terraform init. Each resource type belongs to a single provider.

A Terraform module is a container for multiple resources used together. Modules let you compose and reuse infrastructure patterns. The root module is the working directory; child modules are called via module blocks and can be sourced from the local filesystem, Git, or the Terraform Registry.

Title: terraform init fails with TLS handshake timeout. Body: When running terraform init behind a corporate proxy, the provider download step hangs and times out. Setting HTTPS_PROXY and HTTP_PROXY env vars fixes it.

Title: state lock not released after CTRL-C. Body: If terraform apply is interrupted with CTRL-C during a remote operation, the DynamoDB lock can be left held. Use terraform force-unlock LOCK_ID to recover.

Title: provider plugin not found error. Body: After upgrading Terraform from 0.12 to 1.x, terraform init fails with 'provider not found'. Solution: run terraform init -upgrade to refresh the plugin lock file.

Title: terraform plan shows phantom diff for tag casing. Body: AWS tags appear to change on every plan because the AWS API returns tag keys in inconsistent casing. Workaround: use ignore_changes lifecycle block on the tags attribute.
